seo.title: Cloudflare Workers vs Cloudflare Workers AI vs Cloudflare...
seo.description: A practical comparison of Cloudflare Workers, Cloudflare Workers AI, and Cloudflare AI Gateway for Cloudflare Workers.

content.intro: Compare Cloudflare Workers, Durable Objects, and Pages Functions for edge-native application architecture. Evaluate trade-offs in implementation, scalability, and cost for API, SaaS, and AI workloads.

options.name: Cloudflare Workers
options.tagline: Edge computing runtime for HTTP request handling and API proxies
options.best_for: Low-latency API routing, request transformation, and edge-side computation

options.name: Durable Objects
options.tagline: Stateful object storage with distributed consistency
options.best_for: Coordination, rate limiting, and persistent state management

options.name: Pages Functions
options.tagline: Serverless functions for static site backends
options.best_for: Simple API endpoints with minimal infrastructure overhead

criteria.name: Implementation Complexity
criteria.description: Effort required to implement and debug logic

criteria.name: Lock-in Risk
criteria.description: Dependency on Cloudflare-specific APIs and patterns

criteria.name: Reliability
criteria.description: Uptime guarantees and error handling capabilities

criteria.name: Latency Handling
criteria.description: Performance for cold starts and external dependencies

criteria.name: AI Integration
criteria.description: Support for AI model inference and orchestration

criteria.name: Scalability
criteria.description: Handling of concurrent requests and state

verdict.summary: Workers excel at low-latency API manipulation, Durable Objects provide reliable state management, and Pages Functions offer simple serverless endpoints. Choose Workers for edge compute, Durable Objects for coordination, and Pages Functions for static site APIs.

use_cases.scenario: Building a globally distributed API gateway
use_cases.reason: Leverages edge network for low-latency request routing and transformation

use_cases.scenario: Implementing rate limiting with shared state
use_cases.reason: Provides consistent state management across distributed edges

use_cases.scenario: Creating simple backend APIs for static sites
use_cases.reason: Minimal setup with direct integration to site deployment
Combines edge execution with reliable async processing
